title: Find out if you need a medical exam for your temporary resident application - Canada.ca
content:
## Find out if you need a medical exam for your temporary resident application
- Medical exams for visitors, students and workers
- Find out if you need a medical exam
- Medical exam for permanent residents applicants
- Medical surveillance
If you’re applying to become a temporary resident of Canada, you must have an immigration medical exam (IME) if one of the following conditions apply:
- you want to come to Canada for more than 6 months and have lived in, or travelled to, certain countries or territories, for 6 months or more in a row, in the year before you come to Canada, or
- you are applying for a parent and grandparent super visa , or
- you want to work in a job in which public health must be protected (for example: health care or child care) If public health must be protected you need an exam. It doesn’t matter which countries or territories you’ve visited or how long you plan to stay in Canada.
- If public health must be protected you need an exam. It doesn’t matter which countries or territories you’ve visited or how long you plan to stay in Canada.

## Countries and territories where an IME is required

### The list of countries and territories where an IME is required was updated on November 3, 2025
An IME is now needed if you have lived in or visited:
An IME is no longer needed if you lived in or visited:
- Bosnia and Herzegovina
These changes don’t affect applications submitted before November 3, 2025.
You need an IME if there is a “ yes ” in the second column. Countries and territories are listed alphabetically. Certain overseas locations may also be listed under the country of sovereignty, such as the United Kingdom.
